_doc: DETERMINISTIC semantic golden for the redesign (plan.md §15 Step 0). The Step-5 e2e drives ONE full auto cycle with a STUB llm + STUB env (scripted pass/fail) on `stub_config` and asserts the new system reproduces, for each scenario: the decide() command sequence (§6), per-run/per-task solved-sets + trial-counts (orchestrator §8), and the keep/discard decision (gate §9). This is NOT the real 2-task smoke (config/smoke_config.json), which is plumbing-only and asserts no outcome parity.
_why_not_old_capture: Values are derived from first principles (§6 truth table + §8 scheduling + §9 gate) and GROUNDED in already-passing deterministic tests, not captured from the old control-FSM (which §13 deletes). The old auto loop has no 1:1 command-sequence analog; its preserved orchestrator/gate math is what the cited tests already pin.
_determinism_basis: tests/experiment/test_runner.py (87 pass, 0.69s) proves stub-driven scheduling is deterministic: staggered trial completions + majority watcher reproduce stable trial-counts.

stub_config._note: Synthetic task names only — the stub env returns scripted pass/fail keyed by name; tasks need not be real. Sized so the promotion Fisher is exercisable both ways (>=3 train tasks).

majority._rule: is_majority_solved = solved >= ceil(total/2); for total=3 -> 2. is_majority_decided locks once remaining trials cannot flip the verdict: all-pass and all-fail both lock at 2/3 (metrics.py is_majority_decided).

budget_rules._baseline: RefreshBaseline runs ALL tasks at uniform-full budget k=task_trials=3 (budget_from_baseline(baseline=None)).
budget_rules._candidate_deterministic_solved_by_basel: baseline solved ALL its trials (e.g. 2/2 all-pass) -> deterministic fast-path k=1 (test_run_experiment_runs_single_trial_for_deterministic_baseline_task).
budget_rules._confirm_on_fail: k=1 fast-path whose single trial FAILS expands to k=3, then majority early-stop (test_run_experiment_confirms_on_fail_when_deterministic_trial_fails: fail,fail -> stop at 2, majority False).

scenarios.name: keep__candidate_improves_all_train__no_veto
scenarios.grounding: command_seq=§6 candidate-keep walkthrough; trial-counts=majority early-stop (test_run_panel_early_stops_after_majority_decided) + deterministic k=1 (test_run_experiment_runs_single_trial...); decision=gate.py:136-147 Fisher [[3,0],[0,3]] p=0.10<=0.20.

given.candidate_edit: stub proposer makes a fixed trivial edit to src/harness/core.py that passes validate_candidate (changed paths subset of EDITABLE_PATHS, no task ids) and keeps test_core green.

scenarios.name: discard_at_train__candidate_no_improvement
scenarios.grounding: command_seq=§6 candidate-discard-at-train (test NEVER runs); decision=gate.py:134-135 candidate_solved(0) <= baseline_solved(0) -> 'did not improve'.

given.candidate_edit: fixed trivial edit (passes validate_candidate + test_core), same as the keep scenario.

expect.candidate_test: NOT RUN (gate(train)=discard short-circuits the veto; combine(discard, None)=discard)

scenarios.name: veto_discard__train_keeps_then_test_regresses_via_confirm_on_fail
scenarios.grounding: exercises confirm-on-fail (test_run_experiment_confirms_on_fail: k=1 fail -> expand k=3 -> fail,fail stop at 2) + regression_veto discard (gate.py:131-132 candidate_solved(1) < baseline_solved(2)) + combine(keep, discard)=discard. Same command_seq as keep (RunVeto runs because train kept), but Conclude combines to discard.

v-stable-1._note: confirm-on-fail: k=1 trial fails -> expand k=3 -> fail,fail -> majority-decided False at 2
